answers.value: Domestic cold-water taps in residential units and common areas at Pacific Vista Condominiums, 1840 Marine View Drive, Tamuning, GU 96913
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.value:
Three first-draw drinking water samples were collected from separate locations within the same condominium complex as part of a building permit clearance and routine water quality verification requested by several residents.

Sampling details:
1) Sample PV-01 was collected from Unit 602 (sixth floor, ocean-facing stack) kitchen cold-water tap. The tap had not been used for at least 8 hours prior to collection (overnight stagnation) to provide a first-draw sample representative of potential lead and copper levels at the point of use. Aerator was left in place as instructed in WERI guidance. The sample was collected directly into the sterile container provided by WERI.

2) Sample PV-02 was collected from the main lobby drinking fountain on the ground floor. This fountain is connected downstream of the building’s main pressure-reducing valve and is used frequently by residents and staff. For this sample, the water was allowed to run for approximately 2 minutes prior to collection to obtain a flushed sample representing typical consumer use rather than stagnant water.

3) Sample PV-03 was collected from the fitness center restroom lavatory sink on the second floor, cold side only. The sink is moderately used during morning and evening gym hours but had not been used for at least 3 hours prior to sampling. The purpose of this location is to evaluate water quality at a mid-building elevation and to address a specific resident complaint of intermittent metallic taste and slight discoloration in the mornings.

All samples were collected into pre-labeled, lab-supplied bottles, immediately placed in a cooler with ice packs, and transported directly by car to the WERI laboratory. Chain-of-custody was maintained by the sampler from the time of collection until handoff at the lab reception desk. No field preservatives were added by the sampler; bottles were pre-preserved as provided by WERI where applicable.

Requested analyses: standard drinking water potability panel including total coliform/E. coli, pH, conductivity, turbidity, hardness, iron, manganese, lead, copper, and any other routine parameters typically included for treated municipal drinking water assessments related to building permitting on Guam.

Please notify the point of contact by email if any results exceed Guam EPA or USEPA primary or secondary drinking water standards so that follow-up flushing, plumbing inspection, or additional sampling can be scheduled before final building permit sign-off.
